info.title: Kolega Comply Public API
info.description: Public REST API for Kolega Comply, authenticated via organization API keys.

get.description: List applications for the calling organization.
Get a single application by id.

post.description: Trigger a security scan across all repositories in the application.

get.description: List scan batches for the application.
Get the scan batch you created. ``scan_id`` is the batch id.

Fine-grained progress for a scan batch.

Returns the batch's aggregate counters plus a per-repository breakdown
(including placeholder entries for repos that haven't been materialized
as scans yet when the batch is still QUEUED/CREATING_SCANS). A CLI can
poll this and render a progress bar from ``percent_complete``.

Aggregate findings from every repository in the batch.

``scan_id`` is the batch id from ``POST /scans``. Returns every finding
attributed to this batch across all repositories. For per-repository
slicing, use ``/progress`` to see the breakdown and then filter
findings by ``scan_batch_id`` if needed.
Get a single finding by id.

patch.description: Update the status of a finding (open / resolved / ignored / etc).

post.description: Trigger an autofix run for one or more findings in this application.

get.description: Get a single fix by id.

Get the diff for a fix. Returns ``diff=null`` while the fix is still
running — clients should poll until ``status`` is ``completed``.

Minimal heartbeat for a running fix.

Returns status, timings, agent step count, and a last-activity
timestamp so a CLI can show something like ``"Running... (42s, 12
steps, last update 3s ago)"``. Deliberately does not expose message
content or tool-call details.

post.description: Open pull requests for a completed fix.

get.description:
Return the current-period quota balance for the calling organization.

Check this before triggering a scan or fix — if a counter's
``remaining`` is zero, the POST will 403 ``OPERATION_FAILED`` with
the offending ``quota_type`` in the detail.

PublicFixProgressResponse.title: PublicFixProgressResponse
PublicFixProgressResponse.description:
Minimal heartbeat for a running fix.

Deliberately does not include agent message content or tool-call details
— those are internals that would pin the public contract to the agent
implementation. Clients get status, timings, and an activity pulse.

Batch-level progress + per-repository breakdown. The primary polling

PublicScanResultsResponse.title: PublicScanResultsResponse
PublicScanResultsResponse.description:
Aggregated results from every repository scan in a batch.

Individual per-repository results are no longer part of the public
contract — a consumer that wants to slice by repo should query
``GET /findings?scan_batch_id=…`` (once that filter is exposed) or the
``/progress`` endpoint on the batch.

ScanBatchStatus.title: ScanBatchStatus
ScanBatchStatus.description:
Status of a scan batch.

Lifecycle:
- QUEUED: Batch created, waiting to be picked up
- CREATING_SCANS: Creating scan jobs for each repository
- SCANNING: Scans are running
- ASSESSING: AI assessment in progress
- COMPLETED: All scans and assessments complete
- PARTIAL_FAILURE: Some scans failed, but process completed
- FAILED: Batch-level failure

scan_type.title: Scan Type
scan_type.description: Type of scan: secrets_scan, semgrep_scan, or deep_ai_scan

scans_assessed.title: Scans Assessed
scans_assessed.description: Scans whose findings have been assessed

ScanBatchWithBreakdown.title: ScanBatchWithBreakdown
ScanBatchWithBreakdown.description:
ScanBatch with computed status breakdown for API responses.

Extends ScanBatch with a status_breakdown field containing scan counts by status.
Used in list views to show batch progress without fetching all scans.
